current_visual_risks: Missing or placeholder covers can render as typography-only fallback surfaces in customer-facing cards.
Homepage and library hero type ramps remain large enough to feel claustrophobic on laptop viewports.
Separate shelf placeholder logic can diverge from the shared production cover component.
Adding graphical covers as heavy raster assets could regress Lighthouse and LCP.

scope: detail and reader cover consumers through shared BookCoverImage

performance_budget.fallback_strategy: runtime SVG data URI with no network request and no text baked into art

rollback_plan: Revert BookCoverImage/images resolver changes to restore previous cover selection.
Revert Home/Library/BookCard typography class reductions if visual density regresses.
Remove audit/smoke scripts if they create false positives.
validation_plan: Lighthouse on local production build if tooling/server is available
business_reason: Graphical, content-themed covers improve premium merchandising and trust while avoiding fake availability or heavy decorative debt.
customer_experience_reason: Readers should see collectible literary objects, not plain placeholders, and first-screen typography should feel calm and readable at normal zoom.
risk_notes: Some controlled publications still lack approved cover URLs; resolver fallback prevents typography-only UI but does not replace need for future approved art.
Remote cover byte sizes can be unknown from local audit without live HEAD requests.
Current worktree has unrelated dirty files; selective staging remains required.
